articles.title: What Is Q-Day? The Quantum Threat to Bitcoin Explained
articles.description: Experts warn quantum computers could someday forge Bitcoin’s digital signatures, allowing unauthorized transactions.

articles.title: Bitcoin price tags $62.3K nine-day high after global stocks hit historic record
articles.description: Bitcoin set new highs for July after the Dow Jones and global stocks market cap set new all-time highs before the US Independence Day holiday.

articles.title: American Charged in Israel With Spying for Iran in Exchange for Crypto
articles.description: Israeli prosecutors charged a 21-year-old American man with spying for Iran while studying at a seminary in Jerusalem.

articles.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.title: EU crypto rulebook faces enforcement challenge as MiCA transition ends
articles.description: Lawyers and industry executives expect EU regulators to enforce MiCA differently as unauthorized crypto companies are required to wind down operations.

articles.title: Upbit says it only expressed interest in future OUSD participation
articles.description: Upbit said it only expressed interest in potentially joining the OpenStandard ecosystem in the future, as several South Korean firms distanced themselves from the OUSD initiative.

articles.title: Farage Reported to UK Standards Watchdog Over Alleged Crypto Lobbying
articles.description: A Labour MP says the Reform leader pressed the Bank of England on policy that could enrich his biggest donor, a major Tether investor.

articles.title: Belgian police arrest suspected phishing gang leader tied to $572K theft
articles.description: Belgian authorities say a European phishing gang stole over $572,000 from victims before laundering the proceeds through cryptocurrency.

articles.title: Trump says there is ‘nothing wrong’ with family’s crypto windfall
articles.description: The president reported at least $1.4 billion in crypto income for 2025. He told CNBC he did nothing illegal and was not aware of the extent of his holdings. Critics say he is profiting from the office while his administration writes the industry's rules.

articles.title: StanChart joins ESMA's first MiCA register update since deadline
articles.description: ESMA publishes its first post-deadline MiCA register update, adding 37 crypto-asset service providers, including Standard Chartered and FalconX.

articles.title: Bitcoin ETFs Draw In $222M, Snapping 10-Day Losing Streak
articles.description: Thursday's inflows ended a run that drained $2.7 billion, but analysts warn one green day isn't necessarily a trend reversal.

articles.title: Bitcoin whales bought $16.7 billion of bitcoin in 2 weeks even as ETFs bled a record $4 billion
articles.description: U.S. institutional demand had its worst month ever in June. Large holders absorbed the selling, marking a divergence that has shown up near past cycle bottoms.

articles.title: Bitcoin supply metric prints first 'buy' signal since late 2022 as bear market continues
articles.description: Bitcoin supply dynamics pointed toward the end of the 2026 bear market after the first "buy" signal since November 2022, but analysis warned that BTC price could still go lower.

articles.title: Bitcoin, ether traders aren't fully buying the bounce, options markets show
articles.description: Your day-ahead look for July 3, 2026

articles.title: Zcash’s Ironwood upgrade faces possible delay over infrastructure readiness
articles.description: Shielded Labs said exchanges, mining pools and wallets may need more time to complete Zcash’s Z3 software migration and safely prepare for Ironwood.

articles.title: Crypto bulls on firmer footing as U.S. rate-hike risk recedes
articles.description: Crypto ended the week in a more buoyant state after weak U.S. jobs data reduced the chances of a Fed interest-rate increase and Uniswap gained on a link-up with Robinhood.

articles.title: Defendant files to dismiss New York lawsuit seeking ownership of 39,069 Bitcoin wallets
articles.description: A defendant who owns one of the dormant Bitcoin wallets filed to dismiss the New York case, which seeks ownership of $229 billion worth of Bitcoin considered lost.

articles.title: Memory and semiconductor stocks lose momentum, bitcoin rebounds in sign of changing investor focus
articles.description: After dominating markets in 2026, AI-tied memory and semiconductor stocks are losing momentum, raising the question whether capital will shift back into bitcoin.

articles.title: India's central bank revives push to isolate banks from crypto: Report
articles.description: The Indian central bank reportedly urged lawmakers to keep banks insulated from crypto and private stablecoins while preserving room for regulated tokenization.

articles.title: Tokenization could make finance faster, but also more susceptible to shocks, IMF says
articles.description: Tokenization could make finance faster and cheaper. It also makes it more vulnerable to sudden shocks, the International Monetary Fund said.

articles.title: Irish authorities seize another 500 Bitcoin, bringing 2026 total to 1,500 BTC
articles.description: Irish authorities recovered 500 Bitcoin in criminal proceeds, bringing the total seized by the Criminal Assets Bureau to about $92 million in BTC for 2026.

articles.title: US spot Bitcoin ETFs top $200M in daily inflows for first time since May

articles.title: Live updates: Bitcoin rises above $62,000 as the red hot semiconductor trade starts to fade
Binance says MiCA should be judged by who it licenses, not who it excludes
articles.description: Europe head Gillian Lynch said Binance met Greece's licensing requirements and that the company remains committed to the EU, despite withdrawing its MiCA application days before the July 1 deadline.

articles.title: Teen ‘Scattered Spider’ suspect extradited to US over $8M crypto ransom
articles.description: Peter Stokes, 19, has been charged in the US for allegedly taking part in a hacking group’s unsuccessful $8 million crypto ransom scheme.

articles.title: AI agent development hasn’t accelerated as expected, Zuckerberg says
articles.description: Mark Zuckerberg’s comments came on the same day that Meta expanded its Meta Business Agent globally for businesses on Instagram, Messenger and WhatsApp.

articles.title: Finally. $221 million flow into Bitcoin ETFs, ending a painful 10-day outflow streak
articles.description: Spot ETFs had their strongest inflow day in two months, driven by funds other than BlackRock’s IBIT.

articles.title: XRP bulls test path back toward $1.10 as token zips 4% higher
articles.description: Buyers defended a higher low and pushed XRP toward near-term resistance, though the move still needs a clean break above $1.10 to confirm momentum.

articles.title: Ether and solana extend gains as a short squeeze lifts bitcoin toward $62,000
articles.description: Bearish traders lost $281 million in liquidations over 24 hours, nearly double the longs, as bitcoin pushed to its strongest level in two weeks. Ether is up almost 10% on the week and solana nearly 19% while a rebound in tech stocks eased the pressure from the AI trade.

articles.title: US dominates Polymarket political bets despite geoblock: Report
articles.description: New data from Allium suggests that US users are bypassing geoblocks to bet on markets offered on Polymarket’s global platform.

articles.title: Strategy will be ‘less important’ in Bitcoin after STRC incident: Bitwise
articles.description: Bitwise’s Matt Hougan said Strategy's STRC offer of high yields and low volatility was always a questionable fit for buying Bitcoin, as the cryptocurrency offers neither.

articles.title: A struggling Nasdaq-listed company that tried to copy Saylor's Bitcoin playbook is completely dumping crypto for AI
